name: Community Services of Nevada
description_short: Non-profit housing counselor providing free homebuyer education, foreclosure intervention, and rental assistance in Nevada to help families make sound housing decisions.
description_long:
Community Services of Nevada (CSNV) is a community development non-profit organization based in Nevada dedicated to helping households achieve housing stability and financial security. Founded on the principle of 'changing lives for the better, one household at a time,' CSNV operates as a HUD-approved housing counseling agency offering free and low-cost financial guidance.

CSNV offers comprehensive housing-related counseling services including homebuyer education, foreclosure intervention, post-purchase mortgage counseling, and rental assistance. Their homebuyer program guides borrowers through credit counseling, budgeting, savings strategies, mortgage qualification, and down payment assistance options. They provide both online courses and in-person one-on-one counseling sessions, as well as free webinars. For those facing foreclosure, they offer free orientation sessions two days per week to explore alternatives. Beyond housing, CSNV also provides rental counseling, budgeting services, immigration services, and seasonal low-cost tax preparation.

What distinguishes CSNV is their emphasis on preventing predatory lending and mortgage fraud through personalized counseling relationships rather than transactional services. They specifically market their ability to educate borrowers to 'avoid predatory lending or mortgage scam' and to make 'sound' financial decisions. Their multi-format service delivery—online courses, webinars, in-person counseling, and phone/text appointments—accommodates different learning preferences and accessibility needs. The organization also integrates housing counseling with broader financial literacy and community development services.

CSNV appears to be a legitimate non-profit focused on genuine consumer financial education rather than profit generation. The limitations include geographic restriction to Nevada, potential wait times for appointments given reliance on counselor availability, and services primarily focused on housing rather than general debt management or credit repair. Potential clients should verify current service availability and appointment timelines by contacting the office directly.

When evaluating debt relief companies, consumers should compare settlement programs against alternatives like debt consolidation loans, which combine multiple debts into a single fixed-rate payment. Credit counseling through nonprofit agencies offers free budgeting help without impacting credit scores. For those whose credit has already been damaged, credit repair services can address inaccurate negative items on reports. Personal loans for bad credit may provide funds for debt payoff at lower rates than credit cards, and credit monitoring services help track progress throughout the recovery process. Nonprofit counselors can help consumers evaluate whether an installment loan for debt consolidation makes sense given their income and existing obligations.

pricing.guarantee_details: Contact provider for current pricing and guarantee details.

pros: Free one-on-one homebuyer counseling sessions with trained housing counselors
Free foreclosure intervention counseling available twice weekly to explore alternatives
Multiple service formats including online courses, webinars, in-person classes, and text/phone appointments
Specifically educates borrowers on avoiding predatory lending and mortgage fraud
Covers full homebuying pipeline from credit counseling through down payment assistance and mortgage process
Non-profit model with no financial incentive to steer clients toward loans or products
Free post-purchase counseling for refinancing decisions to prevent mortgage fraud
cons: Services limited to Nevada residents only
Primary focus on housing-related issues rather than general credit repair or debt management
Counselor availability may create appointment delays given reliance on limited staff
No online application process visible—clients must contact via phone, email, or form
Limited detail on immigration services and tax preparation eligibility requirements on website
best_for: First-time homebuyers in Nevada seeking education on the mortgage process and down payment options
Nevada homeowners facing foreclosure exploring alternatives and loss mitigation options
Renters in Nevada seeking budgeting and rental counseling guidance
Borrowers concerned about predatory lending wanting objective housing counseling
diagnosis: CSNV is best for Nevada residents seeking genuinely free, non-profit housing counseling to navigate homebuying or foreclosure situations without sales pressure. The main caveat is that services are geographically limited to Nevada and focused primarily on housing rather than general credit repair, though the organization also offers rental and immigration services.
services: Free homebuyer assistance and education counseling
Budgeting and savings guidance for home purchase
Mortgage pre-qualification and application process education
Down payment assistance program information and navigation
Free rental counseling and budgeting services
Free webinars on down payment assistance and home buying
typical_results_timeline: Results vary by individual situation. Contact the provider to discuss expected timelines for your specific needs.
address: 500 N Rainbow Blvd #300a, Las Vegas, NV 89107
meta_description: Non-profit housing counselor providing free homebuyer education, foreclosure intervention, and rental assistance in Nevada.
